service.name: Environmental Sensing
service.informativeText: Abstract: This service exposes measurement data from an environmental sensor intended for sports and fitness applications. A wide range of environmental parameters is supported. Summary: The Environmental Sensing Service (ESS) exposes measurement data from an environmental sensor intended for sports and fitness applications. A wide range of environmental parameters is supported.

descriptor.name: Environmental Sensing Configuration
descriptor.informativeText: Summary: This definition is shown in the Environmental Sensing Service Section 3.1.2.3

descriptor.name: Characteristic User Description
descriptor.informativeText:
Abstract: The Characteristic User Description descriptor provides a textual user description for a characteristic value.
Summary: If the Writable Auxiliary bit of the Characteristics Properties is set then this descriptor is written. Only one User Description descriptor exists in a characteristic definition.

descriptor.name: Valid Range
descriptor.informativeText:
Abstract: The Characteristic Valid Range descriptor is used for defining the range of a characteristics. Two mandatory fields are contained (upper and lower bounds) which define the range.
Summary: If the Characteristic Value to which this descriptor is attached has a fixed exponent, then the values in this descriptor have the same exponent. The first value in the Valid Range descriptor represents the lower inclusive value of the range. The second value represents the higher inclusive value of the range. The data type and units for lower inclusive value and the upper inclusive value are identical to the data type and units of the characteristic for which it is used.
Examples: When used with the Measurement Interval characteristic, the Valid Range descriptor is formatted using a uint16. If the valid range has a Minimum Value of 10 minutes (600 seconds) and a Maximum Value of 2 hours (7200 seconds) the value of the Valid Range descriptor would be expressed as: 0x58 0x02 0x20 0x1C
A characteristic that is formatted using a nibble with a fixed decimal-exponent that has a Valid Range of 2 to 13 has a Valid Range descriptor defined as: 0x02 0x0D
A characteristic value that is formatted using an sint16 with a fixed exponent of -1 that has a Valid Range of -40 to +85 is expressed as: 0x70 0xFE 0x52 0x03

descriptor.name: Environmental Sensing Configuration
descriptor.informativeText: Summary: This definition is shown in the Environmental Sensing Service Section 3.1.2.3

descriptor.name: Characteristic User Description
descriptor.informativeText:
Abstract: The Characteristic User Description descriptor provides a textual user description for a characteristic value.
Summary: If the Writable Auxiliary bit of the Characteristics Properties is set then this descriptor is written. Only one User Description descriptor exists in a characteristic definition.

descriptor.name: Valid Range
descriptor.informativeText:
Abstract: The Characteristic Valid Range descriptor is used for defining the range of a characteristics. Two mandatory fields are contained (upper and lower bounds) which define the range.
Summary: If the Characteristic Value to which this descriptor is attached has a fixed exponent, then the values in this descriptor have the same exponent. The first value in the Valid Range descriptor represents the lower inclusive value of the range. The second value represents the higher inclusive value of the range. The data type and units for lower inclusive value and the upper inclusive value are identical to the data type and units of the characteristic for which it is used.
Examples: When used with the Measurement Interval characteristic, the Valid Range descriptor is formatted using a uint16. If the valid range has a Minimum Value of 10 minutes (600 seconds) and a Maximum Value of 2 hours (7200 seconds) the value of the Valid Range descriptor would be expressed as: 0x58 0x02 0x20 0x1C
A characteristic that is formatted using a nibble with a fixed decimal-exponent that has a Valid Range of 2 to 13 has a Valid Range descriptor defined as: 0x02 0x0D
A characteristic value that is formatted using an sint16 with a fixed exponent of -1 that has a Valid Range of -40 to +85 is expressed as: 0x70 0xFE 0x52 0x03

descriptor.informativeText: Summary: This definition is shown in the Environmental Sensing Service Section 3.1.2.3

descriptor.name: Valid Range
descriptor.informativeText:
Abstract: The Characteristic Valid Range descriptor is used for defining the range of a characteristics. Two mandatory fields are contained (upper and lower bounds) which define the range.
Summary: If the Characteristic Value to which this descriptor is attached has a fixed exponent, then the values in this descriptor have the same exponent. The first value in the Valid Range descriptor represents the lower inclusive value of the range. The second value represents the higher inclusive value of the range. The data type and units for lower inclusive value and the upper inclusive value are identical to the data type and units of the characteristic for which it is used.
Examples: When used with the Measurement Interval characteristic, the Valid Range descriptor is formatted using a uint16. If the valid range has a Minimum Value of 10 minutes (600 seconds) and a Maximum Value of 2 hours (7200 seconds) the value of the Valid Range descriptor would be expressed as: 0x58 0x02 0x20 0x1C

A characteristic value that is formatted using an sint16 with a fixed exponent of -1 that has a Valid Range of -40 to +85 is expressed as: 0x70 0xFE 0x52 0x03

A characteristic that is formatted using a nibble with a fixed decimal-exponent that has a Valid Range of 2 to 13 has a Valid Range descriptor defined as: 0x02 0x0D
A characteristic value that is formatted using an sint16 with a fixed exponent of -1 that has a Valid Range of -40 to +85 is expressed as: 0x70 0xFE 0x52 0x03

A characteristic value that is formatted using an sint16 with a fixed exponent of -1 that has a Valid Range of -40 to +85 is expressed as: 0x70 0xFE 0x52 0x03

descriptor.name: Environmental Sensing Configuration
descriptor.informativeText: Summary: This definition is shown in the Environmental Sensing Service Section 3.1.2.3

descriptor.name: Characteristic User Description
descriptor.informativeText:
Abstract: The Characteristic User Description descriptor provides a textual user description for a characteristic value.
Summary: If the Writable Auxiliary bit of the Characteristics Properties is set then this descriptor is written. Only one User Description descriptor exists in a characteristic definition.

descriptor.name: Valid Range
descriptor.informativeText:
Abstract: The Characteristic Valid Range descriptor is used for defining the range of a characteristics. Two mandatory fields are contained (upper and lower bounds) which define the range.
Summary: If the Characteristic Value to which this descriptor is attached has a fixed exponent, then the values in this descriptor have the same exponent. The first value in the Valid Range descriptor represents the lower inclusive value of the range. The second value represents the higher inclusive value of the range. The data type and units for lower inclusive value and the upper inclusive value are identical to the data type and units of the characteristic for which it is used.
Examples: When used with the Measurement Interval characteristic, the Valid Range descriptor is formatted using a uint16. If the valid range has a Minimum Value of 10 minutes (600 seconds) and a Maximum Value of 2 hours (7200 seconds) the value of the Valid Range descriptor would be expressed as: 0x58 0x02 0x20 0x1C
A characteristic that is formatted using a nibble with a fixed decimal-exponent that has a Valid Range of 2 to 13 has a Valid Range descriptor defined as: 0x02 0x0D
A characteristic value that is formatted using an sint16 with a fixed exponent of -1 that has a Valid Range of -40 to +85 is expressed as: 0x70 0xFE 0x52 0x03
